verses.commentary: Very truly I tell you Pharisees, anyone who does not enter the sheep pen by the gate, but climbs in by some other way, is a thief and a robber. Shepherd imagery begins. Gate represents legitimate entry. Climbers are illegitimate. Pharisees are thieves by implication.
Authorized access. Jesus is the true shepherd.
Sheep know the shepherd's voice. Personal knowledge. Individual calling. Leading, not driving.
Sheep follow by recognition. Trust through familiarity.

But they will never follow a stranger; in fact, they will run away from him because they do not recognize a stranger's voice. Discernment protects. Unfamiliar voice triggers flight. True sheep distinguish truth from error.
Spiritual blindness persists. They don't recognize themselves as false shepherds.
Therefore Jesus said again, Very truly I tell you, I am the gate for the sheep. Third 'I AM' statement. Metaphor shifts. Jesus is both shepherd and gate. Entrance to salvation. Access point.
Pretenders exposed. True sheep reject imposters. Historical claimants failed.
Salvation only through Jesus. Freedom and provision. Coming and going: security and liberty. Pasture: spiritual nourishment.
Thieves take life. Jesus gives abundant life. Fullness versus depletion. Life to the full: overflowing, exceeding, eternal.

I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down His life for the sheep. Fourth 'I AM' statement. Kalos (καλός), good, beautiful, noble. Defining characteristic: self-sacrifice. Death for the sheep. The cross foreshadowed.
No ownership, no commitment. Danger reveals character. Mercenary flees. Sheep abandoned. False leaders exposed in crisis.
Economic relationship versus love. Indifference versus devotion. Pharisees are hirelings.
I am the good shepherd; I know My sheep and My sheep know Me. Mutual knowledge. Intimate relationship. Recognition is reciprocal. Personal, not impersonal.

Just as the Father knows Me and I know the Father, and I lay down My life for the sheep. Divine analogy. Jesus-Father relationship mirrors shepherd-sheep relationship. Mutual knowledge in Trinity. Death is voluntary sacrifice.
Universal mission. Jews and Gentiles unified. One flock, not two. One shepherd: Jesus. Ephesians 2:14-16 fulfilled.
The reason My Father loves Me is that I lay down My life only to take it up again. Love grounded in obedience. Voluntary death. Resurrection foreseen. Death temporary, resurrection certain.

No one takes it from Me, but I lay it down of My own accord. I have authority to lay it down and authority to take it up again. This command I received from My Father. Sovereignty in death. Not victim but volunteer. Authority over life and death. Resurrection power inherent. Father's command obeyed.
Jesus polarizes. No neutrality possible.
Many of them said, He is demon-possessed and raving mad. Why listen to Him? Dismissal. Insanity charged. They reject without engagement. Demonization as explanation.

But others said, These are not the sayings of a man possessed by a demon. Can a demon open the eyes of the blind? Reasonable defense. Demons don't heal. Chapter 9 miracle remembered. Works validate words.
. Winter: cold, dark. Symbolic atmosphere.
Sheltered walkway. Accessible location.

The Jews who were there gathered around Him, saying, How long will You keep us in suspense? If You are the Messiah, tell us plainly. Demand for clarity. Impatient inquiry. They want explicit claim. Actually, He has been plain; they refuse to see.

I did tell you, but you do not believe. The works I do in My Father's name testify about Me. Repeated testimony. Unbelief is the issue, not lack of clarity. Works are witnesses. Miracles validate identity.
Not all are His sheep. Belief distinguishes sheep from non-sheep. Divine election implied.
True disciples recognize and respond.
Eternal life given. Never perish: absolute guarantee. Divine grip unbreakable. Perseverance of the saints.
Father's hand holds. Greater than all threats. Divine sovereignty protects. Election ensures preservation.
I and the Father are one. Hen esmen (ἕν ἐσμεν), we are one. Unity of essence. Not identical persons but unified nature. Deity claimed. Strongest assertion of divinity yet.
They understand the claim. Blasphemy perceived. Leviticus 24:16 invoked.
But Jesus said to them, I have shown you many good works from the Father. For which of these do you stone Me? Calm question. Works listed. Which one deserves death? Ironic inquiry.

We are not stoning You for any good work, but for blasphemy, because You, a mere man, claim to be God. Charge explicit. They correctly understand His claim. Mere man asserting deity. From their perspective, capital offense.
Jesus answered them, Is it not written in your Law, I have said you are gods? Psalm 82:6 quoted. Elohim (אֱלֹהִים) used for human judges. If Scripture calls humans gods...
If He called them gods, to whom the word of God came, and Scripture cannot be set aside. Argument from lesser to greater. Scripture is unbreakable. Those who received God's word called gods.

What about the One whom the Father set apart as His very own and sent into the world? Why then do you accuse Me of blasphemy because I said, I am God's Son? Logic: if mere humans called gods, how much more the One sent by God? Son of God is lesser claim than what He could make.
Test by works. If no divine works, reject Him. Fair standard.

But if I do them, even though you do not believe Me, believe the works, that you may know and understand that the Father is in Me, and I in the Father. Works as evidence. Start with signs if words fail. Mutual indwelling claimed. Progressive understanding: know and understand.
Divine protection. They cannot arrest Him. Sovereignty over timing.
Return to origins. Where ministry began publicly. Safe haven.

And many people came to Him. They said, Though John never performed a sign, all that John said about this man was true. Testimony validated. John's witness confirmed. No miracles but true prophecy. Preparation bears fruit.
Witness leads to faith. Preparation creates receptivity. Belief multiplies in fertile ground.
